questions.stem:
—________ you ever ________ to the new science museum?
—Yes, I ________ there with my classmates last Friday.
questions.explanation:
本题考查现在完成时与一般过去时的区别。
现在完成时（have been to）表示"曾经去过某地（已回来）"，强调经历；一般过去时（went）表示"过去某个具体时间发生的动作"，与last Friday这一过去时间状语连用。
A选项第一空错误，ever是现在完成时标志词；B选项第二空have gone to表示"去了某地（未回）"，与答句逻辑矛盾；D选项第一空错误，ever不与一般过去时连用。
解题技巧：看到ever/never/just/already/yet等词用现在完成时；看到yesterday/last week/in 2020等具体过去时间用一般过去时。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The student ________ won the first prize in the English speech contest is from Class 3.
questions.explanation:
本题考查定语从句关系代词的用法。
先行词是The student（指人），且关系代词在从句中作主语，应使用who。
A选项which用于指物；C选项whom用于指人但作宾语；D选项whose表示"……的"，在从句中作定语。
解题技巧：指人用who/whom/that，指物用which/that；作主语用who/which，作宾语用whom/who/which，作定语用whose。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: These old books ________ to the school library by the graduates yesterday afternoon.
questions.explanation:
本题考查一般过去时的被动语态。
主语These old books与return是被动关系（书被归还），时间状语yesterday afternoon表示过去，因此用一般过去时的被动语态were returned。
A选项是主动语态；C选项是现在完成时主动语态；D选项是一般现在时被动语态，时态不符。
解题技巧：被动语态基本结构为"be+过去分词"，根据时间状语确定be动词的时态。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem:
—Listen! Someone is knocking at the door. Is it Tom?
—It ________ be him. He has gone to Shanghai on business.
questions.explanation:
本题考查情态动词表推测的用法。
根据后句"He has gone to Shanghai"（他去上海出差了，还没回来），可推断"不可能是他"。can't表示"不可能"，用于否定推测。
A选项must表示"一定是"，与句意相反；B、C选项may/might表示"可能是"，把握性较弱，但此处是确定性的否定排除。
解题技巧：must be（一定是），can't be（不可能是），may/might be（可能是）。根据后文给出的确定信息选择相应的推测语气。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: To protect the environment, we should find ways to ________ waste into useful things instead of throwing it away.
questions.explanation:
本题考查词义辨析。
recycle表示"回收利用"，符合句意"把废物变成有用的东西"。
B选项reduce意为"减少"；C选项reuse意为"重复使用"，但通常指直接使用而不经过加工转变；D选项repeat意为"重复"。
解题技巧：根据语境"into useful things"（转变成有用的东西）判断是"回收加工"的过程，而非简单的减少或重复使用。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: It is ________ to stay up late playing computer games. It is bad for your health.
questions.explanation:
本题考查形容词词义及构词法。
根据后句"bad for your health"可知此处应填"不健康的"。un-是否定前缀，unhealthy表示"不健康的"。
A选项意为"健康的"，与句意相反；C选项healthful较正式，意为"有益健康的"；D选项healthless不是标准英语词汇。
解题技巧：un-是常见否定前缀（unhappy, unhealthy, unfair），根据上下文逻辑（熬夜玩游戏=不健康）选择带否定前缀的词。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: We need to ________ some good ideas to solve the problem of plastic pollution.
questions.explanation:
本题考查动词短语辨析。
come up with意为"想出（主意、办法等）"，符合句意"想出好主意"。
B选项catch up with意为"追上，赶上"；C选项keep up with意为"跟上，保持同步"；D选项put up with意为"忍受，容忍"。
解题技巧：come up with与ideas/plans/solutions搭配，表示"提出、想出"。记住up with类短语的差异：come up with（想出），keep up with（跟上），put up with（忍受）。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.explanation:
本题考查逻辑关系与副词辨析。
前文讲"人们过去把垃圾扔进海里"，后文讲"现在他们开始分类回收"，前后是对比转折关系，且强调"取而代之"的做法，用Instead（相反，取而代之）。
A选项However表示转折，但通常用逗号隔开，且侧重"然而"；C选项Therefore表示因果；D选项Besides表示递进。
解题技巧：关注上下文逻辑关系，看到"过去……现在……"的对比结构，且后文是替代前文的做法，选Instead。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last year, our school started an environmental protection project called "Green Campus". The goal was to reduce waste and save energy in our daily school life.

In the past, students used to throw all kinds of rubbish into the same bin without thinking. (8)______, now we have four different colored bins in every classroom. They are for paper, plastic, food waste, and other waste. At first, many students thought it was troublesome to sort the waste. (9)______, as time went by, we realized it was not that difficult.

Our science teacher, Mr. Li, played an important role in this project. He often told us interesting stories about how recycled materials could be (10)______ into new products. For example, old newspapers could become exercise books again. This made us understand the importance of recycling.

The most exciting part was the "DIY Workshop" held last month. Students brought old clothes and bottles from home. With the help of volunteers, we (11)______ them into beautiful pencil cases and shopping bags. Everyone felt proud when they saw their handmade items.

Now, the "Green Campus" project has become a great success. Not only has it helped us develop good habits, (12)______ it has also raised our awareness of environmental protection. We hope more schools can join us to make the earth a better place.

questions.explanation:
本题考查转折连词的用法。
前文说"起初许多学生认为分类麻烦"，后文说"随着时间推移，我们意识到并不难"，前后是明显的转折关系，用But连接。
A选项And表示并列或递进；C选项Or表示选择；D选项So表示结果。
解题技巧：看到"At first...（起初）"和后面的对比（意识到并不难），判断为转折关系，选择But。注意However和But的区别：However后面通常有逗号，且多用于句首或句中；But直接连接句子，无逗号。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last year, our school started an environmental protection project called "Green Campus". The goal was to reduce waste and save energy in our daily school life.

In the past, students used to throw all kinds of rubbish into the same bin without thinking. (8)______, now we have four different colored bins in every classroom. They are for paper, plastic, food waste, and other waste. At first, many students thought it was troublesome to sort the waste. (9)______, as time went by, we realized it was not that difficult.

Our science teacher, Mr. Li, played an important role in this project. He often told us interesting stories about how recycled materials could be (10)______ into new products. For example, old newspapers could become exercise books again. This made us understand the importance of recycling.

The most exciting part was the "DIY Workshop" held last month. Students brought old clothes and bottles from home. With the help of volunteers, we (11)______ them into beautiful pencil cases and shopping bags. Everyone felt proud when they saw their handmade items.

Now, the "Green Campus" project has become a great success. Not only has it helped us develop good habits, (12)______ it has also raised our awareness of environmental protection. We hope more schools can join us to make the earth a better place.

questions.explanation:
本题考查动词词义辨析及固定搭配。
change...into...是固定搭配，意为"把……变成……"，符合句意"回收材料如何被变成新产品"。
A选项divided常与into搭配，但意为"把……分成"；C选项thrown into意为"扔进"；D选项collected不与into搭配表示"变成"。
解题技巧：关注动词与into的搭配，change into（变成），divide into（分成）。根据语境"old newspapers could become exercise books"判断是"转变"的过程。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last year, our school started an environmental protection project called "Green Campus". The goal was to reduce waste and save energy in our daily school life.

In the past, students used to throw all kinds of rubbish into the same bin without thinking. (8)______, now we have four different colored bins in every classroom. They are for paper, plastic, food waste, and other waste. At first, many students thought it was troublesome to sort the waste. (9)______, as time went by, we realized it was not that difficult.

Our science teacher, Mr. Li, played an important role in this project. He often told us interesting stories about how recycled materials could be (10)______ into new products. For example, old newspapers could become exercise books again. This made us understand the importance of recycling.

The most exciting part was the "DIY Workshop" held last month. Students brought old clothes and bottles from home. With the help of volunteers, we (11)______ them into beautiful pencil cases and shopping bags. Everyone felt proud when they saw their handmade items.

Now, the "Green Campus" project has become a great success. Not only has it helped us develop good habits, (12)______ it has also raised our awareness of environmental protection. We hope more schools can join us to make the earth a better place.

questions.explanation:
本题考查动词短语搭配。
turn...into...是固定短语，意为"把……变成……"，与上文的"change into"同义复现，符合句意"把旧衣服和瓶子变成漂亮的笔袋和购物袋"。
B选项bought（买）、C选项found（发现）、D选项cut（切割）均不能与into构成"转变"含义的搭配。
解题技巧：完形填空注重上下文呼应，上文提到"change into new products"，此处是具体例子，因此选择同义词turn into。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last year, our school started an environmental protection project called "Green Campus". The goal was to reduce waste and save energy in our daily school life.

In the past, students used to throw all kinds of rubbish into the same bin without thinking. (8)______, now we have four different colored bins in every classroom. They are for paper, plastic, food waste, and other waste. At first, many students thought it was troublesome to sort the waste. (9)______, as time went by, we realized it was not that difficult.

Our science teacher, Mr. Li, played an important role in this project. He often told us interesting stories about how recycled materials could be (10)______ into new products. For example, old newspapers could become exercise books again. This made us understand the importance of recycling.

The most exciting part was the "DIY Workshop" held last month. Students brought old clothes and bottles from home. With the help of volunteers, we (11)______ them into beautiful pencil cases and shopping bags. Everyone felt proud when they saw their handmade items.

Now, the "Green Campus" project has become a great success. Not only has it helped us develop good habits, (12)______ it has also raised our awareness of environmental protection. We hope more schools can join us to make the earth a better place.

questions.explanation:
本题考查固定句型搭配。
not only...but (also)...是固定句型，意为"不但……而且……"，符合句意"不但帮助我们养成好习惯，而且提高了环保意识"。
A选项and不能与not only构成固定搭配；C选项or表示选择；D选项so表示结果。
解题技巧：记住固定搭配not only...but also...，其中also可以省略。这是中考常见句型，表示递进关系。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last year, our school started an environmental protection project called "Green Campus". The goal was to reduce waste and save energy in our daily school life.

In the past, students used to throw all kinds of rubbish into the same bin without thinking. (8)______, now we have four different colored bins in every classroom. They are for paper, plastic, food waste, and other waste. At first, many students thought it was troublesome to sort the waste. (9)______, as time went by, we realized it was not that difficult.

Our science teacher, Mr. Li, played an important role in this project. He often told us interesting stories about how recycled materials could be (10)______ into new products. For example, old newspapers could become exercise books again. This made us understand the importance of recycling.

The most exciting part was the "DIY Workshop" held last month. Students brought old clothes and bottles from home. With the help of volunteers, we (11)______ them into beautiful pencil cases and shopping bags. Everyone felt proud when they saw their handmade items.

Now, the "Green Campus" project has become a great success. Not only has it helped us develop good habits, (12)______ it has also raised our awareness of environmental protection. We hope more schools can join us to make the earth a better place.

questions.stem: What problem did Dr. Chen's team try to solve?
options: A. People spent too much time on phones.
B. Traditional batteries were too expensive.
C. Electronic waste caused serious pollution.
D. Phones were easily damaged by water.
questions.explanation:
本题考查细节理解。
根据文章第一段"Every year, millions of phones are thrown away, causing serious environmental problems. Dr. Chen and his team wanted to change this situation."可知，Dr. Chen的团队试图解决电子垃圾（废弃手机）造成的污染问题。
A选项是人们使用手机时间长短，文中未提及；B选项传统电池价格，文中未提及；D选项手机防水问题，这是后文提到的材料特性，不是问题本身。
解题技巧：细节题要精准定位关键词（Dr. Chen's team），在原文中找到对应句子，注意同义替换（change this situation指代解决前文提到的污染问题）。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解
questions.group_passage:
Every year, millions of phones are thrown away, causing serious environmental problems. These old phones contain harmful chemicals that can pollute the soil and water. Dr. Chen and his team wanted to change this situation.

After five years of research, they developed a new kind of phone case made from plant fibers (纤维). Unlike plastic cases that take hundreds of years to break down, these plant-based cases can decompose (分解) naturally in just two years. "It's like returning the material to nature," said Dr. Chen.

What's more surprising is that the case can actually help plants grow. When buried in soil, it releases nutrients (养分) that make the earth more fertile. The team has tested this with tomato plants, and the results were amazing. The plants grew 20% faster than those in normal soil.

The only disadvantage is that the case costs 30% more than plastic ones. However, Dr. Chen believes that as more people buy them, the price will drop. "We are not just making a phone case," he said. "We are sacrificing a little money today to save our planet tomorrow."

Several phone companies have already shown interest in this invention. If everything goes well, these eco-friendly cases will be available in stores next spring.

questions.stem: What can we infer from the passage about the phone case?
options: A. It is already sold in many shops.
B. It will never become cheaper.
C. It can completely replace plastic cases now.
D. It may become popular if the price goes down.
questions.explanation:
本题考查推理判断。
根据最后一段"If everything goes well, these eco-friendly cases will be available in stores next spring"（明年春天才会上市）可排除A；根据第四段"as more people buy them, the price will drop"（买的人越多价格会下降）可排除B；根据第四段"The only disadvantage is that the case costs 30% more"可知目前成本较高，难以完全替代塑料，排除C。
D选项正确，因为文章提到"as more people buy them, the price will drop"，可推断出如果价格下降，会吸引更多人购买，从而变得流行。
解题技巧：推理题要基于原文信息合理推断，不能选原文直接陈述的事实（如A是原文直接说的明年才上市），也不能过度推断（如C的completely）。关注条件状语从句（if）表达的可能性。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断
questions.group_passage:
Every year, millions of phones are thrown away, causing serious environmental problems. These old phones contain harmful chemicals that can pollute the soil and water. Dr. Chen and his team wanted to change this situation.

After five years of research, they developed a new kind of phone case made from plant fibers (纤维). Unlike plastic cases that take hundreds of years to break down, these plant-based cases can decompose (分解) naturally in just two years. "It's like returning the material to nature," said Dr. Chen.

What's more surprising is that the case can actually help plants grow. When buried in soil, it releases nutrients (养分) that make the earth more fertile. The team has tested this with tomato plants, and the results were amazing. The plants grew 20% faster than those in normal soil.

The only disadvantage is that the case costs 30% more than plastic ones. However, Dr. Chen believes that as more people buy them, the price will drop. "We are not just making a phone case," he said. "We are sacrificing a little money today to save our planet tomorrow."

Several phone companies have already shown interest in this invention. If everything goes well, these eco-friendly cases will be available in stores next spring.

questions.stem: What does the underlined word "sacrificing" in the last paragraph probably mean?
options: A. Giving up something valuable for a better purpose.
B. Spending a lot of money on useless things.
C. Saving money for future use.
D. Asking others to pay for the cost.
questions.explanation:
本题考查词义猜测。
根据上下文"We are sacrificing a little money today to save our planet tomorrow"，今天牺牲一点钱（多付30%成本），明天拯救地球，是为了更好的目的而放弃眼前利益。
B选项"花在无用事物上"与拯救地球矛盾；C选项"存钱"与"花钱"相反；D选项"让别人付钱"文中未提及。
解题技巧：通过上下文逻辑关系猜测词义，sacrifice意为"牺牲、献出"，指为了更重要的目标（save our planet）而放弃某些东西（a little money）。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测
questions.group_passage:
Every year, millions of phones are thrown away, causing serious environmental problems. These old phones contain harmful chemicals that can pollute the soil and water. Dr. Chen and his team wanted to change this situation.

After five years of research, they developed a new kind of phone case made from plant fibers (纤维). Unlike plastic cases that take hundreds of years to break down, these plant-based cases can decompose (分解) naturally in just two years. "It's like returning the material to nature," said Dr. Chen.

What's more surprising is that the case can actually help plants grow. When buried in soil, it releases nutrients (养分) that make the earth more fertile. The team has tested this with tomato plants, and the results were amazing. The plants grew 20% faster than those in normal soil.

The only disadvantage is that the case costs 30% more than plastic ones. However, Dr. Chen believes that as more people buy them, the price will drop. "We are not just making a phone case," he said. "We are sacrificing a little money today to save our planet tomorrow."

Several phone companies have already shown interest in this invention. If everything goes well, these eco-friendly cases will be available in stores next spring.

questions.stem: What is the best title for the passage?
options: A. The History of Mobile Phones
B. A New Green Phone Case
D. The Harm of Electronic Waste
questions.explanation:
本题考查主旨大意/最佳标题。
文章主要介绍Dr. Chen团队研发的一种新型环保手机壳，包括其材料、优点（可分解、滋养土壤）、缺点（价格较高）以及市场前景。
A选项"手机历史"只是背景；C选项"如何种番茄"只是实验细节；D选项"电子垃圾的危害"只是开头引子。B选项"一种新型绿色手机壳"概括了全文核心内容。
解题技巧：最佳标题要涵盖全文核心话题，不能过大（如D只涉及第一段），也不能过小（如C只涉及第三段），要准确反映文章说明对象及其特征（new, green/eco-friendly）。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意
questions.group_passage:
Every year, millions of phones are thrown away, causing serious environmental problems. These old phones contain harmful chemicals that can pollute the soil and water. Dr. Chen and his team wanted to change this situation.

After five years of research, they developed a new kind of phone case made from plant fibers (纤维). Unlike plastic cases that take hundreds of years to break down, these plant-based cases can decompose (分解) naturally in just two years. "It's like returning the material to nature," said Dr. Chen.

What's more surprising is that the case can actually help plants grow. When buried in soil, it releases nutrients (养分) that make the earth more fertile. The team has tested this with tomato plants, and the results were amazing. The plants grew 20% faster than those in normal soil.

The only disadvantage is that the case costs 30% more than plastic ones. However, Dr. Chen believes that as more people buy them, the price will drop. "We are not just making a phone case," he said. "We are sacrificing a little money today to save our planet tomorrow."

Several phone companies have already shown interest in this invention. If everything goes well, these eco-friendly cases will be available in stores next spring.

options: A. First of all, make a study plan and stick to it.
B. However, don't forget to leave some time for rest.
C. As a result, you will feel stressed and tired all the time.
D. On the other hand, doing sports can help you study better.
E. Then, try to use your free time wisely.
questions.explanation:
本题考查段落主题句与逻辑顺序。
空格后第二段开始讲"Second"（第二），说明此处应是"第一"。A选项"First of all"（首先）符合顺序逻辑，引出制定学习计划这一首要建议。
B选项However表转折，用于对比；C选项As a result表结果；D选项On the other hand表并列对比；E选项Then表承接（然后），通常不用于段首总起。
解题技巧：关注后文的"Second"，反推前文应为"First"或"First of all"，形成"首先……其次……"的逻辑链条。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many students find it hard to balance study and after-school activities. They often feel stressed because they have too much homework but also want to join clubs or play sports. Here are some tips to help you manage your time better.

(17)______ Write down what you need to do each day and check the list when you finish. This helps you stay organized and avoid wasting time.

(18)______ For example, you can review English words while waiting for the bus, or read a book during lunch break. Small pieces of time add up to big progress.

(19)______ You don't need to study for five hours without stopping. Taking short breaks every 45 minutes can improve your concentration and memory.

(20)______ Physical exercise not only keeps you healthy but also makes your brain more active. So find a sport you enjoy and do it regularly.

Remember, balancing study and play is important for your growth. Use these tips and you will enjoy a colorful school life.

options: A. First of all, make a study plan and stick to it.
B. However, don't forget to leave some time for rest.
C. As a result, you will feel stressed and tired all the time.
D. On the other hand, doing sports can help you study better.
E. Then, try to use your free time wisely.
questions.explanation:
本题考查过渡句与例证关系。
空格后"For example"举例说明如何利用碎片时间（等车时背单词、午餐时读书），因此空格处应提出"利用空闲时间"的建议。E选项"Then, try to use your free time wisely"（然后，试着明智地利用空闲时间）符合语境。
A选项是第一条建议（已用于17题）；B选项讲休息；C选项是负面结果；D选项讲运动（与后文运动建议重复）。
解题技巧：关注"For example"信号词，前文应为总述观点，后文为具体例子。Then表示顺序上的"接下来"，承接前文的First of all。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many students find it hard to balance study and after-school activities. They often feel stressed because they have too much homework but also want to join clubs or play sports. Here are some tips to help you manage your time better.

(17)______ Write down what you need to do each day and check the list when you finish. This helps you stay organized and avoid wasting time.

(18)______ For example, you can review English words while waiting for the bus, or read a book during lunch break. Small pieces of time add up to big progress.

(19)______ You don't need to study for five hours without stopping. Taking short breaks every 45 minutes can improve your concentration and memory.

(20)______ Physical exercise not only keeps you healthy but also makes your brain more active. So find a sport you enjoy and do it regularly.

Remember, balancing study and play is important for your growth. Use these tips and you will enjoy a colorful school life.

options: A. First of all, make a study plan and stick to it.
B. However, don't forget to leave some time for rest.
C. As a result, you will feel stressed and tired all the time.
D. On the other hand, doing sports can help you study better.
E. Then, try to use your free time wisely.
questions.explanation:
本题考查转折与补充建议。
空格后讲"不需要连续学习五小时""每45分钟休息可以提高专注力"，因此空格处应建议"休息/放松"。B选项"However, don't forget to leave some time for rest"（然而，别忘了留些时间休息）符合语境，与前文的紧张学习形成转折。
A、E已使用；C选项是负面结果，与后文积极建议不符；D选项讲运动，与休息不同。
解题技巧：However表示语义转折，前文讲如何利用时间学习，此处转折讲也要休息，形成"学习-休息"的平衡建议。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many students find it hard to balance study and after-school activities. They often feel stressed because they have too much homework but also want to join clubs or play sports. Here are some tips to help you manage your time better.

(17)______ Write down what you need to do each day and check the list when you finish. This helps you stay organized and avoid wasting time.

(18)______ For example, you can review English words while waiting for the bus, or read a book during lunch break. Small pieces of time add up to big progress.

(19)______ You don't need to study for five hours without stopping. Taking short breaks every 45 minutes can improve your concentration and memory.

(20)______ Physical exercise not only keeps you healthy but also makes your brain more active. So find a sport you enjoy and do it regularly.

Remember, balancing study and play is important for your growth. Use these tips and you will enjoy a colorful school life.

options: A. First of all, make a study plan and stick to it.
B. However, don't forget to leave some time for rest.
C. As a result, you will feel stressed and tired all the time.
D. On the other hand, doing sports can help you study better.
E. Then, try to use your free time wisely.
questions.explanation:
本题考查并列建议与主题一致性。
空格后内容讲"体育锻炼不仅保持健康还能让大脑更活跃""找一项喜欢的运动定期做"，因此空格处应提出"运动"建议。D选项"On the other hand, doing sports can help you study better"（另一方面，做运动能帮助你学得更好）符合语境。
On the other hand用于引出并列的另一方面建议（前文讲学习计划和休息，此处讲运动），形成全面平衡的建议体系。
解题技巧：关注后文关键词"Physical exercise"，与D选项中的"doing sports"完全对应，属于"总-分"结构。

questions.group_passage:

(17)______ Write down what you need to do each day and check the list when you finish. This helps you stay organized and avoid wasting time.

(18)______ For example, you can review English words while waiting for the bus, or read a book during lunch break. Small pieces of time add up to big progress.

(19)______ You don't need to study for five hours without stopping. Taking short breaks every 45 minutes can improve your concentration and memory.

(20)______ Physical exercise not only keeps you healthy but also makes your brain more active. So find a sport you enjoy and do it regularly.

Remember, balancing study and play is important for your growth. Use these tips and you will enjoy a colorful school life.
